title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends on fuel transition, shipyard actions, government, ports, financing and industry players. The scale of shipping's green transition has been put into sharp relief with new data from Germany's University of Applied Sciences showing the global shipping industry will require the equivalent of the world's entire current renewable energy demand to replace fossil fuel use. Read More

The key indicator to watch is consumer resilience
Historically, Bear Markets last for a few months to a couple of years.

children.summary:
The scale of shipping's green transition has been put into sharp relief with new data from Germany's University of Applied Sciences showing the global shipping industry will require the equivalent of the world's entire current renewable energy demand to replace fossil fuel use. Read More

children.title: The key indicator to watch is consumer resilience
section_path: The key indicator to watch is consumer resilience
children.section_path_text: The key indicator to watch is consumer resilience
children.summary: Historically, Bear Markets last for a few months to a couple of years. The quicker tightening slows, or ends, the quicker we will see an end to volatility and the return of market bullishness.

children.title: A risk-on tone across markets helped pushed the commodity complex higher
section_path: A risk-on tone across markets helped pushed the commodity complex higher
children.section_path_text: A risk-on tone across markets helped pushed the commodity complex higher
children.summary: A risk-on tone across markets helped pushed the commodity complex higher. Industrials led the gains amid increasing hopes of stimulus boosting economic activity.

children.title: The central government continues to work to stimulate the economy
section_path: The central government continues to work to stimulate the economy
children.section_path_text: The central government continues to work to stimulate the economy
children.summary: As we have highlighted to our clients in our recent Weekly China Reports, Chinese banks issued only 645 trillion yuan in new loans in April. This has marked a month-on-month decline of 2.49 billion yuan (-80%) and a year-on-year decline of 825 billion yuan (-56%).

children.title: The grim economic outlook in China continued to weigh on sentiment in commodity markets
section_path: The grim economic outlook in China continued to weigh on sentiment in commodity markets
children.section_path_text: The grim economic outlook in China continued to weigh on sentiment in commodity markets
children.summary:
Base metals led the complex lower, while gains in oil were offset by falls in coal in the energy sector. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-Ck-sZCQVoYY.jpg | assets/2022-05-29_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-ck-szcqvoyy_66d61b900173.jpg

children.title: New round of downgrades to growth projections among economists and other China watchers
section_path: New round of downgrades to growth projections among economists and other China watchers
children.section_path_text: New round of downgrades to growth projections among economists and other China watchers
children.summary: China's recent release of disappointing data for industrial production during April has triggered a new round of downgrades to growth projections among economists and other China watchers.

children.title: China's coal production has nevertheless stayed quite strong
section_path: China's coal production has nevertheless stayed quite strong
children.section_path_text: China's coal production has nevertheless stayed quite strong
children.summary:
As we have highlighted to our clients in Commodore's recent Weekly Dry Bulk Reports, China's coal production in April totaled 362.8 million tons. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-AByHM2Q7wIk.jpg | assets/2022-05-29_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-abyhm2q7wik_a380bb6772d2.jpg
Image reference: unsplash-image-2OGEg25-kCY.jpg | assets/2022-05-29_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-2ogeg25-kcy_213a28c50b1a.jpg

children.title: Fresh demand in the Atlantic
children.summary: As tr ade flows continue to change as the Ukraine-Russia war unfolds, we look at how the US has increased shipments to Europe across several dry bulk commodities and what it means for dry bulk markets.

children.title: In contrast to widespread assumptions and news pieces
section_path: In contrast to widespread assumptions and news pieces
children.section_path_text: In contrast to widespread assumptions and news pieces
children.summary:
Image reference: 1 (1).jpg | assets/2022-05-29_weekly-insights-review_img_128129_ca68145c6c11.jpg
Curtailments to domestic use, as struggling product exports limit refining operations, have been a crucial supporting factor for crude exports. Meanwhile, sanctions are unlikely to hinder Russian players from exporting oil over the coming months more than they already do.
